mcqs.question: Which of the following is a core component responsible for converting a physical phenomenon into an electrical signal in an IoT device?
mcqs.explanation: A sensor is specifically designed to detect and measure changes in the environment (e.g., temperature, light) and convert them into a digital or analog signal that the processor can understand.

mcqs.question: What is a key differentiating factor between a Microcontroller Unit (MCU) and a Microprocessor Unit (MPU) in the context of IoT?

options.text: An MPU is always more powerful than an MCU.
An MCU integrates memory and peripherals on a single chip, while an MPU requires external support chips.
An MCU can connect to the internet, while an MPU cannot.
An MPU is used for sensing, while an MCU is used for actuation.

mcqs.explanation: This is the fundamental architectural difference. An MCU is a self-contained system-on-a-chip (SoC), making it simpler and more power-efficient. An MPU is a standalone CPU that relies on external components for functionality, allowing for more power and flexibility.

mcqs.question: For a large-scale agricultural project requiring thousands of soil moisture sensors to send small amounts of data over long distances from remote fields, which connectivity technology is MOST suitable?
mcqs.explanation: LoRaWAN is a Low-Power Wide-Area Network (LPWAN) protocol designed precisely for this scenario: long-range communication (several kilometers) with very low power consumption, perfect for battery-operated sensors in remote locations. Wi-Fi and BLE have short range, and NFC requires very close proximity.

mcqs.question: Which layer of the standard IoT architecture model is primarily composed of the physical IoT devices themselves, including their sensors and actuators?
mcqs.explanation: The four-layer model consists of: Perception Layer (devices/sensors), Network Layer (connectivity/transport), Processing Layer (data analysis/cloud), and Application Layer (user apps/services). The Perception Layer is the physical foundation.

mcqs.question: Why is power management a critical design constraint for most IoT devices?

options.text: To make the devices as heavy as possible.
To reduce the computational speed for better accuracy.
To ensure the device can operate for extended periods, often on a battery, without frequent maintenance.
To increase the cost of the device for higher profit margins.

mcqs.explanation: Many IoT devices are deployed in hard-to-reach locations or in such large numbers that frequent battery replacement or charging is impractical and expensive. Therefore, optimizing for low power consumption is paramount for feasibility and cost-effectiveness.
